$comment: Compatibility manifest for Microsoft.Android.Sdk.Linux pack versions.
Each entry maps a pack version to the shim release tag whose tarball
bit-for-bit shims the same upstream x86_64 binaries. Microsoft frequently
republishes the workload pack with the same host binaries (only the .NET
managed bits change), so most pack versions can be served from a single
shim release.
Identity entries (e.g. "36.1.53" → "36.1.53") are omitted: install-shims.sh
tries the literal pack version first and only consults this manifest as a
fallback when no release exists at that exact tag.
An entry is added here when the watcher (.github/workflows/watch-nuget.yml)
verifies that the new pack version's host binaries (aapt2, libMono.Unix.so,
libZipSharpNative-3-3.so, and the 6 binutils/bin/ entries) are byte-identical
to those in an existing shim release. If any sha256 drifts, the watcher opens
an issue requesting a new release build (or, for binutils drift, a manual
review of the LLVM mapping under .binutils below).
The .binutils block describes the host-installed Phase 6 toolchain mapping
consumed by install-shims.sh. See docs/llvm-toolchain.md for rationale.

anchors.$comment: sha256 of the upstream x86_64 binaries that each shim release replaces. The watcher uses these to detect drift.

$comment: Host-installed Phase 6 toolchain mapping consumed by install-shims.sh.
Strategy: instead of bundling ~50 MB of aarch64 LLVM/binutils binaries in
the release tarball, install-shims.sh symlinks the 6 x86_64 entries in
<pack>/tools/Linux/binutils/bin/ to host-installed equivalents. The user
is expected to provide LLVM via apt (one-liner documented below).
Minimum LLVM major: 15 — the .NET Android SDK 36.x emits LLVM IR with
opaque pointers (`ptr`), introduced in LLVM 15. Bullseye-stock LLVM 11
fails. Validated working: LLVM 19.1.7 from apt.llvm.org/bullseye.
preferred_llvm_majors is consulted in order; first hit wins. To pin a
specific LLVM major on a host, point install-shims.sh at it explicitly
via --llvm-major.
host_install_recipe is the apt one-liner the install script prints when
no acceptable LLVM is found on the host. {codename} is substituted at
runtime from `lsb_release -cs`.
host_install_recipe: echo 'deb http://apt.llvm.org/{codename}/ llvm-toolchain-{codename}-19 main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/llvm-19.list
sudo apt-get install -y llvm-19 lld-19 binutils

mapping.$comment: Pack-relative path → host-path template. {llvm} is replaced with the detected LLVM major.
